'''House Pelagia''' was a [[Tapani Noble Houses|coalition of noble families]] that controlled the [[Pelagia Province]] of the [[Tapani sector/Legends|Tapani sector]]. House Pelagia held greater esteem for honor and dignity than the other houses of the [[Expanse]]. Assassinations were strongly discouraged, and lying was undesirable. As a result, Pelagian nobles tended to be masters of spin and were adept at manipulating the truth to suit their needs.

==History==
The first colonists from the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core]] settled on [[Pelagon]] approximately [[13,000 BBY]] and were initially governed from the Pelagian capital world by the [[Pella Compact]], the original council that led the colony ships to Pelagon. As more worlds were colonized, power shifted away from Pelagon to clan capitals.

[[File:Pelagian_Lady.jpg|thumb|left|170px|A Pelagian Lady]]
House Pelagia was once one of the most powerful of all houses of the Tapani sector, and they often controlled the balance of power in the [[Great Council (Tapani Federation)|Great Council]] along with their historical enemies [[House Mecetti]]. A number of Pelagian families had bloodlines strong in [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], and several were members of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. House Pelagia allied themselves with the Jedi against House Mecetti and the Mecrosa Order during the [[Cleansing of the Nine Houses]] about 4000 years BBY (year 8,720 in Tapani sector Calendar).<ref name="LE" /> During this time the [[Pelagia Duplex Command Assault Gunship]] entered service to protect the Tapani sector from the chaos of the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]].<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic Handbook">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic Handbook]]''</ref>

When [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] branded the Jedi traitors of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] and issued [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]], which begun the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Jedi Purge]], the Jedi bloodlines in House Pelagia were rooted out and eliminated.

Circa [[16 BBY/Legends|16 BBY]], motivated by the fear of [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] reprisals and jealous of House Pelagia's power and influence, the other houses joined with Pelagia's old enemy, Mecetti, in opposing House Pelagia militarily for the first time. The initial battles were ferocious, and for a while it appeared the House Pelagia might be able to fend off the combined forces of the other house fleets. Eventually several Mecetti agents were able to disable Pelagon's [[Planetary shield/Legends|planetary shield]], and the once mighty house was defeated. [[House Cadriaan]] prevented Mecetti from totally destroying the house, but it only retained three of its original 11 systems; the other eight were ceded to [[House Calipsa]], and its capital world of Pelagon was devastated. All but two of Pelagon's great platforms were destroyed, and its oceans were contaminated from the bombing. Only ten percent of Pelagon's species escaped extinction. The house lost seventy percent of its votes on the Great Council.

After the [[Mecetti Purge]], House Pelagia had to put all of its resources into surviving, and with the help of House Cadriaan it did. House Pelagia was careful not to overtly make any significant political moves, aware that another attack would most likely destroy the house.

After the purge many noble Pelagian families went to the [[Freeworlds Territory]] to start new lives while others were received into other houses as second-class aristocrats. Some of these families secretly maintained their old loyalty to the fallen house and acted as a spy network routing information and resources to Pelagia.

The House Pelagia starship ''[[Regal Destiny]]'' disappeared during the Mecetti Purge. It was rumored that the ship contained treasures from the fallen house. The treasures turned out to be the long-lost [[Trad Paddox|brother]] and [[Verinia Paddox|sister]] of the Pelagian High Lord Theux Paddox, who were betrayed by a Mecetti spy posing as a crewman. He poisoned the ship's air supply with a bio-agent, forcing the [[Winslo|children's governess]] to put them in stasis. The ''Regal Destiny'' and the two hibernating members of the Theux family were later discovered by the crew of the mining survey ship ''[[Bright Seeker]]'' shortly after the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]].<ref name="Tapani Sector Instant Adventures">''[[Tapani Sector Instant Adventures]]''</ref>

After the death of Emperor Palpatine, House Pelagia began the long road to recovery.

==Pelagia Province==
{{Main|Pelagia Province}}
The Mecetti Purge of House Pelagia left only three systems in the Pelagian Province: [[Bethal system/Legends|Bethal system]], [[Obelia system/Legends|Obelia system]] and [[Pelagon system/Legends|Pelagon system]], containing two habitable worlds, [[Bethal]] and the capital world [[Pelagon]]. Eight systems—[[Betolio system/Legends|Betolio]], [[Grella system/Legends|Grella]], [[Lorenz system/Legends|Lorenz]], [[Kamper system/Legends|Kamper]], [[New Javis system/Legends|New Javis]], [[Setolio system/Legends|Setolio]] and [[Netolio system/Legends|Netolio]]—were ceded to House Calipsa during the fall.<ref name="LE" />

==Assets==
[[File:Pelagia_Holocron.jpg|thumb|right|280px|House Pelagia [[Holocron/Legends|Holocron]]]]
After the fall, Pelagia had very little in the way of assets. Many businesses left the house when it was defeated, seeking a more stable environment. The [[Pelagon Aquatic Components Corporation]], however, remained based on Pelagon.

The best asset that Pelagia controlled was the [[Shapani Bypass]], which was the fastest [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperroute]] to Cadriaan. Pelagia ensured that its enemies, including House Calipsa, paid high tariffs for passing through. At one time Calipsa was banned from Pelagian space and therefore isolated from the Shapani Run.

House Pelagia preserved some of its Jedi heritage after the purge including a collection of Jedi [[Holocron/Legends|holocrons]]. This secret was closely guarded and only known by a handful of trusted individuals. During the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]] the [[Mecrosa Order]]'s top agent, [[Nevil Tritum]], retrieved [[Holocron of King Adas|the Sith holocron]] of the ancient [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith]] [[Sith King|King]] [[Adas]] from House Pelagia's possession.<ref name="Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties">{{InsiderCite|88|Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties}}</ref>

===[[House Defense Forces]] (0 BBY to 3.5 ABY)===
Over the approximately twenty years since its fall, Pelagia had been quietly rebuilding its defensive forces. By placing orders through its allies in the House Cadriaan government it successfully prevented Mecetti agents from discovering the full extent of the re-armament program. The Herglic [[Melchi]], captain of a [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|Nebulon-B medical frigate]] called the ''[[Benevolence]]'', used a mobile medical relief unit as cover for smuggling military supplies to House Pelagia.

During the period between [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]] and [[3 ABY/Legends|3.5 ABY]] the Pelagian navy consisted of the following [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]] although it had placed orders for more:
*2 [[Tapani-class assault frigate|''Tapani''-class frigates]] – bought secondhand<ref name="LE" />
*5 [[Rendili StarDrive]] [[Neutron Star-class bulk cruiser/Legends|''Neutron Star''-class bulk cruisers]] – bought secondhand<ref name="LE" />
*''[[Pelagia's Moon|Pelagia Star]]'' (''Pelagia's Moon'') – refitted during the Galactic Civil War<ref name="LE" />
*[[Guardian-class light cruiser]] ''[[Reprise]]''<ref name="Tapani Sector Instant Adventures" />

Although Pelagia lost a great deal of its military power during the purge, it did retain its powerful intelligence and diplomatic assets. There were many agents of House Pelagia across the sector, many directed against the old enemy Mecetti.

==Politics==
House Cadriaan was Pelagia's closest friend and ally in the sector. It ensured Pelagia's survival and helped in its rebuilding and rearmament efforts.<ref name="LE" />

After the fall of House Pelagia the Great Council on [[Procopia/Legends|Procopia]] was controlled by an a coalition of House Mecetti, House Calipsa, and [[House Reena/Legends|House Reena]]. House Mecetti had always been Pelagia's most implacable enemy. At this time Calipsa and Reena were enemies by their alliance with Mecetti.<ref name="LE" />

Before the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], under the leadership of High Lord [[Theus Paddox]], House Pelagia participated in secret talks with [[House Melantha|Melantha]] and [[House Barnaba|Barnaba]]. Such an alliance would break Mecetti's control of the Great council and therefore the sector. House Melantha had historically been one of Pelagia's allies in opposing Mecetti, but it was always an alliance of convenience.<ref name="LE" />

Although House Pelagia did not associate itself with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebellion]], a Rebel Alliance cell that called itself the [[Pelagia Freedom Force]] was led by a native of Pelagon, [[Zanthe Nadia]].<ref name="LE" />
